steps.title: Compute the result
steps.content: Simplify: $$f(0) = 270(1) = 270.$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice D is correct. The value of $f\left(0\right)$ is the value of $f\left(x\right)$ when $x=0$. Substituting $0$ for $x$ in the given function yields $f\left(0\right)=270{\left(0.1\right)}^{0}$, or $f\left(0\right)=270\left(1\right)$, which is equivalent to $f\left(0\right)=270$. Therefore, the value of $f\left(0\right)$ is $270$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $x$, not $f\left(x\right)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This is the value of $f\left(1\right)$, not $f\left(0\right)$.
